---
title: "Project2"
output: html_document
---

# Monte Carlo Feature Selection

Identify the set of relevant features that would be used for classification

# Load
```{r}

#install.packages("rJava")
#install.packages("rmcfs")
library(rJava)
library(rmcfs)
library(R.ROSETTA)

set.seed(9054)

# Load data
df <- read.csv("data/clean/train_data.csv",
               colClasses = "character",
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

df_test <- read.csv("data/clean/test_data.csv",
                    colClasses = "character",
                    stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Drop column ID
data_train <- subset(df, select = -id )
data_test <- subset(df_test, select = -id )

# Inspect basic structures of train and test
#str(data_train)
#str(data_test)
```

# MCFS
```{r}
# Perform monte carlo feature selection (random reducts)
rank_feats <- mcfs(class ~ ., data_train, projections = 1500, projectionSize = 0.1, splits = 5, splitSetSize = 500, cutoffPermutations = 20, threadsNumber = 8)

# Fetch results
train_feats <- rank_feats$RI[1:rank_feats$cutoff_value,]

# Keep only relevant features
sel_data_train <- data_train[, colnames(data_train) %in% c(train_feats$attribute, "class"), drop = FALSE]
sel_data_test <- data_test[, colnames(data_test) %in% c(train_feats$attribute, "class"), drop = FALSE]

# convert from char to factor
sel_data_train <- as.data.frame(sel_data_train)
sel_data_train[] <- lapply(sel_data_train, as.factor)

sel_data_test <- as.data.frame(sel_data_test)
sel_data_test[] <- lapply(sel_data_test, as.factor)

dim(sel_data_train)
dim(sel_data_test)
```

# Rosetta Cross Val
```{r}
ros <- rosetta(sel_data_train,
              reducer = "Johnson",
              JohnsonParam = list(Modulo = TRUE, BRT = TRUE, BRTprec = 0.7, Precompute = FALSE, Approximate = TRUE, Fraction = 1),
              reducerDiscernibility = "Object",
              classifier = "StandardVoter",
              underSample = FALSE,
              roc = TRUE, discrete=TRUE, clroc = "Human")

rules <- ros$main
quality <- ros$quality

rec <- recalculateRules(sel_data_train, rules, discrete=TRUE)
rec <- rec[rec$pValue <= 0.05, ]

cat("Johnson - Number of rules:", nrow(rules), "\n")
print(quality)

# print top 3 rules for human
rlsHuman <- viewRules(rules[rules$decision=="Human",])
print(rlsHuman[1:3,])

sig_rules <- rules[rules$pValue < 0.05, ]
cat("Significant rules (p < 0.05):", nrow(sig_rules), "/", nrow(rules), "\n\n")
tabS <- table(sig_rules$decision)
print(tabS)

plotMeanROC(ros)
```

# Rosetta Test Run
```{r}
truth <- sel_data_test[, "class"]
test_features <- subset(sel_data_test, select = -class)

# use rec from train to infer on test
pred <- predictClass(test_features, rec, discrete = TRUE, validate = TRUE, defClass = truth)

library(caret)
RBM_pred <- factor(pred$out$predictedClass, levels = levels(truth))
cm <- confusionMatrix(RBM_pred, truth)
print(cm)
```

# Important Features
```{r}
gf <- getFeatures(rec)
print(gf$features)

featsAvian  <- gf$features$Avian          # Features occurring in significant avian rules.
featsHuman <- gf$features$Human         # Features occurring in significant human rules.

commonfeats <- intersect(featsHuman, featsAvian)
commonfeats

uniquefeatsHuman <- setdiff(featsHuman, featsAvian)
uniquefeatsAvian <- setdiff(featsAvian, featsHuman)
uniquefeatsHuman
uniquefeatsAvian
```

# Top Rules per Class
```{r}
# Top rule for class 0
rules_0 <- rec[rec$decision == "Avian", ]
top_0 <- which.min(rules_0$pValue)
cat("Top rule for class 0 - p =", rules_0$pValue[top_0], "\n")
print(viewRules(rules_0[top_0, ]))

# Top rule for class 1
rules_1 <- rec[rec$decision == "Human", ]
top_1 <- which.min(rules_1$pValue)
cat("\nTop rule for class 1 - p =", rules_1$pValue[top_1], "\n")
print(viewRules(rules_1[top_1, ]))
```

# Clustering Model Rules
```{r}
#Hierarchical clustering of model rules
#subsetting the significant rules from the recalculated rules
sig_rec_rules <- rec[rec$pValue < 0.05,]
clusterRules(sel_data_train,sig_rec_rules,fontsize = 5)
```

# Visunet
```{r}
library(RCy3)
library(csVisuNet)
cytoscapePing ()
vis <- visunetcyto(ros$main)
```

# VisuArc
```{r}
... replace with proper VisuArc cell
```
